data.title: Bitcoin Whale Faces Significant Losses Amid Market Volatility
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, on-chain analyst Yu Jin has reported substantial losses for Bitcoin whale James Wynn due to market fluctuations. Over the past 24 hours, Wynn's long positions have incurred losses totaling $12 million, affecting his principal investment.

The reduction in margin has prevented Wynn from increasing his positions after a decline. His Bitcoin long position has decreased from $760 million yesterday morning to $279 million currently. Additionally, his remaining margin of $5.9 million is insufficient to support his previous positions, which were worth several billion dollars.

Currently, Wynn holds a 40x leveraged long position of 2,636 BTC, valued at $279 million. The opening price was $107,993, with a liquidation price of $105,067.

data.title: Ethereum Sidechain Games Face Shutdown Amid Financial Struggles
data.description:
According to PANews, two games based on the Ethereum sidechain Ronin, 'Realms of Alurya' and 'Wonder Wars,' are set to cease operations due to financial difficulties. Developer Hello Monster announced the decision to terminate 'Wonder Wars' operations and cancel its token issuance after failing to meet critical operational metrics and secure funding. Meanwhile, 'Realms of Alurya' is being taken offline following the sudden termination of cooperation by its publisher, Treasure, and a subsequent funding shortfall. The team is currently seeking solutions.

This development continues the trend of crypto game shutdowns in 2025, with previous projects like 'Nyan Heroes' and 'Ember Sword' also halting operations for similar reasons. Industry analysts highlight that challenges in securing funding and a lack of sufficient players are accelerating market consolidation.

data.title: Trump: The International Trade Court's ruling is politicized, and I hope the Supreme Court can overturn it
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Trump published a super long post in response to recent court rulings on his trade policy, saying that the U.S. International Trade Court incredibly ruled against the United States on the tariffs that the United States desperately needs, but fortunately, the 11-judge panel of the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit has just suspended the ruling. The ruling of the U.S. International Trade Court is so wrong and so politicized. I hope the Supreme Court will quickly and decisively overturn this terrible, country-threatening decision. This terrible decision shows that I must get the approval of Congress to impose these tariffs. 
In other words, hundreds of politicians will sit in Washington for weeks, even months, trying to come to a conclusion on how to charge countries that practice unfair trade practices against us. If this is allowed, it will completely destroy the power of the president-the presidency will be very different from before. (Jin Shi)

data.title: Coinbase to reopen office in San Francisco
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: San Francisco Mayor Daniel Lurie said on the X platform: "Coinbase is returning to San Francisco after leaving it four years ago and has opened a 150,000 square foot office in Mission Rock. San Francisco is an ideal place to start and grow a company. Welcome back." In response, Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong said: "We are very happy to reopen an office in San Francisco. Although there is still a lot of work to do to improve the city."

data.title: US SEC clarifies that some PoS staking activities do not constitute securities transactions
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to Eleanor Terrett, a crypto journalist, the SEC’s Division of Corporate Finance clarified that staking activities on some Proof-of-Stake blockchains do not constitute securities transactions.
This new guidance follows the guidance on some Proof-of-Work mining activities in March this year, covering self-staking, staking-as-a-service, and additional services such as slashing protection and custom rewards.
“Providing security itself is not a security,” said Hester Peirce, Commissioner of the SEC.

data.title: Tether CEO: The company holds more than 100,000 bitcoins and 50 tons of gold
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Paolo Ardoino, CEO of stablecoin issuer Tether, disclosed at the Bitcoin 2025 Conference that the company currently holds more than 100,000 bitcoins (worth more than $10 billion) and more than 50 tons of gold. According to the latest financial report, as of the end of the first quarter of 2025, Tether held bitcoins worth more than $7 billion (based on the then $83,000 per coin), and physical gold bars worth more than $6 billion.
Ardoino said that Tether achieved a profit of about $13 billion in 2024, making it the most profitable company in the crypto industry. Regarding the decision to hold gold, he explained that "gold is not competing with Bitcoin, but with fiat currency." Currently, USDT is the world's largest dollar stablecoin. (The Block)

data.title: Micro-Brazil International plans to raise up to $300 million to build XRP reserves to support its global payment solutions
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News - Mobile travel service provider Webus International Limited is exploring a financing strategy of up to US$300 million to establish an XRP reserve to support its global payment solutions through diversified non-equity financing tools. The strategy may include the use of available cash reserves, bank loans, shareholder guarantees, and third-party credit support from traditional banks. By adopting these non-dilutive financing methods, the company aims to maintain capital flexibility, support long-term growth, and protect shareholder value while advancing its digital asset and cross-border payment strategic goals.
